title: Decision Record
description: A single decision captured by an agent, a human, or the synthesis/orchestrator layer. Records are the queryable substrate beneath the human-readable DECISIONS.md log; future sessions ground new choices in this corpus instead of re-deriving from first principles.
$comment: EXPANSION JOINTS — the fields most likely to evolve as the decomposition model settles are `phase`, `level`, `tags`, and `evidence.kind`. These are kept deliberately loose (open strings / extensible enums) so they can grow WITHOUT a breaking schema bump. The structural fields (id, type, status, relationships) are strict on purpose: changing them is a versioned, deliberate act. additionalProperties is false at the root so any genuinely new field forces a schema version bump — that is the 'version the contract, not the implementation' discipline, encoded.

schema_version.description: The contract version this record was written against. Consumers use it to migrate.

provenance.description: Optional link back to the plan task this decision came from, so the timeline view can group decisions by phase/task. Added in 0.2.0; optional, so hand-logged records stay lightweight.

id.description: Stable identifier. Prefix encodes origin: ADR=agent, HDR=human, SDR=synthesis. Must agree with `type` (enforced below).

type.description: agent = an executing worker paused, researched, and decided. human = a person's review/annotation. synthesis = a Fable/orchestrator-level choice (decomposition, routing, arbitration).

status.description: Lifecycle. `superseded` matters over multi-year codebases: decisions are revisited, not deleted.

confidence.description: Agent's self-assessed certainty. Low/medium can gate automatic escalation to human review.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601. Primary axis for the timeline view.

phase.$comment: EXPANSION JOINT — loose string, not an enum. Add phases freely as the decomposition model evolves.

level.$comment: EXPANSION JOINT — the level-of-resolution axis Colin wants for filtering. Loose by design.

tags.description: Taxonomy for precedent queries, e.g. ['concurrency','schema-design']. Keep a shared vocabulary so `query by tag` actually retrieves relevant precedent instead of drowning in unrelated decisions.
tags.$comment: EXPANSION JOINT — the controlled vocabulary lives in convention/docs, not in this schema, so it can grow without a bump.

title.description: One-line statement of what was decided. This is the DECISIONS.md index entry.

context.description: Why this decision point arose — the constraint or problem that forced a choice.

options.description: Alternatives genuinely considered. This is the part code can never reconstruct retroactively.

research.description: Passive research the agent did before deciding — what it explored/tested. Distinct from `reasoning` (what it concluded).

reasoning.description: The 'why', in plain language. Synthesized for the rich view later; readable by humans now.

evidence.description: Proof the decision holds: tests, metrics, diffs, PRs, benchmarks.

steelman.description: PHI-028: the strongest honest case FOR each rejected option, written by the decider. Counters self-justification; required by convention for AgDR-class records (enforced in review, not schema).

blast_radius.description: PHI-028: what this decision touches and how expensive reversal is — enables async review at the PR gate.

depends_on.description: DAG edges to prior decisions this one builds on. The inverse ('informs') is DERIVED at build time, never stored, to avoid bidirectional drift.

supersedes.description: Decisions this one replaces. Captures the years-long lineage of how thinking evolved.

superseded_by.description: Set when a later decision replaces this one. Old record stays; it is never deleted.

feedback.description: The review/evolution trail. A decision is a LIVING artifact: human stamps and later annotations append here, so a reader years on sees the original choice AND how it was refined.

role.description: Optional, e.g. 'coder', 'reviewer', 'EM'. Useful audit context at L&W.

action.description: What the agent investigated, e.g. 'stress-tested both under 10k concurrent users'.

kind.$comment: EXPANSION JOINT — loose so new proof types don't need a bump.

ref.description: Pointer: test path, commit SHA, PR URL, metric name, etc.

action.description: What the reviewer did. Drives the status transition.

note.description: The feedback itself — recorded so the team's evolving judgment is preserved, not lost in a PR thread.
